title: Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics
description: Science fiction author Isaac Asimov introduces the Three Laws of Robotics in his short story 'Runaround', forming the earliest theoretical foundation for AI safety and regulation.

title: Turing Test Proposed
description: Alan Turing publishes 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence', establishing the foundational criteria for machine intelligence and sparking early debates on algorithmic accountability.

title: The Dartmouth Workshop
description: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' is officially coined at a summer research project at Dartmouth College, establishing AI as a distinct academic discipline and laying the groundwork for future governance.

title: The ALPAC Report
description: The US Automatic Language Processing Advisory Committee publishes a highly critical report slashing government funding for machine translation, establishing an early precedent for government audits of AI feasibility.

title: The Lighthill Report
description: Sir James Lighthill's highly critical evaluation of AI research is published by the UK Science Research Council, leading to massive government funding cuts and triggering the 'First AI Winter'.

title: Fifth Generation Computer Systems Project
description: Japan's Ministry of International Trade and Industry launches a massive, state-sponsored initiative to leapfrog the West in AI technology, sparking a global 'arms race' in AI research funding.

title: The Alvey Programme
description: In direct response to Japan's Fifth Generation initiative, the UK government launches a massive £350 million cooperative research project to aggressively bolster domestic AI capabilities.

title: Strategic Computing Initiative
description: The US Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) launches a massive $1 billion initiative to integrate advanced AI, expert systems, and autonomy into military applications.

title: EU Data Protection Directive
description: The European Union adopts Directive 95/46/EC. It is one of the first international legal frameworks to explicitly address the right of individuals to not be subject to fully automated decision-making.

title: South Korea's Robot Ethics Charter
description: The South Korean government drafts one of the world's first state-backed ethical frameworks designed to establish norms and prevent human abuse by autonomous robots, heavily inspired by Asimov's Laws.

title: DoD Directive 3000.09
description: The U.S. Department of Defense issues a landmark directive establishing policy for the development and use of autonomous and semi-autonomous functions in weapon systems, mandating appropriate levels of human judgment.

title: 'Losing Humanity' Report
description: Human Rights Watch and Harvard Law School launch the report 'Losing Humanity: The Case Against Killer Robots,' significantly elevating global discourse and advocating for a preemptive ban on fully autonomous weapons.

title: First UN CCW Meeting on LAWS
description: The United Nations Convention on Certain Conventional Weapons (CCW) holds its first informal meeting of experts in Geneva to discuss questions related to Lethal Autonomous Weapons Systems (LAWS).

title: Pan-Canadian AI Strategy
description: Canada becomes the first country in the world to launch a national AI strategy, investing heavily in establishing AI infrastructure, talent retention, and deep learning research.

title: New Generation AI Development Plan
description: China releases its comprehensive national strategy to become the world's primary AI innovation center by 2030, sparking a global focus on national AI competitiveness and tech infrastructure.

title: UAE Strategy for Artificial Intelligence
description: The UAE launches the 'UAE Strategy for Artificial Intelligence 2031' and appoints the world's first Minister of State for AI, aiming to boost government performance and establish a hub for AI innovation.

title: Asilomar AI Principles
description: Created by the Future of Life Institute, a set of 23 principles designed to ensure that AI remains beneficial to humanity, endorsed by thousands of AI researchers globally.

title: India National Strategy for AI
description: NITI Aayog releases India's National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence, establishing the foundation for India's AI governance and its core focus on 'AI for All' and social inclusion.

title: GDPR Automated Decision-Making Rules
description: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) becomes enforceable in the EU. Article 22 specifically regulates automated decision-making and profiling, granting users the 'right to an explanation'.

title: US Executive Order 13859
description: The 'Maintaining American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence' Executive Order is signed, establishing the American AI Initiative to direct federal agencies to prioritize AI R&D and regulation.

title: Singapore National AI Strategy
description: Singapore launches its first National AI Strategy (NAIS) to leverage AI for societal and economic benefit, aiming to be a leader in developing and deploying scalable AI solutions.

title: OECD AI Principles
description: The OECD adopts its Principles on Artificial Intelligence, the first intergovernmental standard on AI, promoting innovative and trustworthy AI that respects human rights and democratic values.

title: Global Partnership on AI (GPAI)
description: The Global Partnership on AI is launched as an international and multistakeholder initiative to guide the responsible development and use of AI based on human rights and democratic values.

title: South Korea National AI Strategy
description: Building on the Presidential Commission on the Fourth Industrial Revolution, South Korea officially enacts its National Artificial Intelligence Strategy (2021-2025) to govern domestic AI deployment.

title: Brazilian AI Strategy (EBIA)
description: The Brazilian Artificial Intelligence Strategy (EBIA) is released, aiming to guide public policies, foster innovation, and address ethical concerns related to AI development in South America.

title: EU AI Act Proposed
description: The European Commission proposes the Artificial Intelligence Act, introducing a strict risk-based approach to AI regulation and banning 'unacceptable risk' applications like social scoring.

title: China Algorithm Provisions
description: The Administrative Provisions on Algorithm Recommendation for Internet Information Services take effect, establishing a foundational algorithm registry system targeting how information is pushed to users.

title: EU AI Liability Directive Proposed
description: The European Commission proposes the AI Liability Directive, designed to establish rules on non-contractual civil liability for damage caused by AI, complementing the broader AI Act.

title: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
description: The US White House Office of Science and Technology Policy releases a non-binding blueprint outlining five core principles to guide the design, use, and deployment of automated systems.

title: China Deep Synthesis Provisions
description: China enacts rules governing deepfakes, text-to-speech, and AI-synthesized content, requiring content labeling to prevent deception and ensuring adherence to state security guidelines.

title: Rwanda National AI Policy
description: Rwanda approves its National AI Policy, aiming to leverage AI for economic growth, digital inclusion, and to firmly position the country as a leading regional AI innovator in Africa.

title: Hiroshima AI Process (HAIP)
description: Launched by G7 leaders at the Hiroshima Summit to promote safe, secure, and trustworthy AI. It establishes an international, voluntary code of conduct for organizations developing advanced systems.

title: China Generative AI Measures
description: The Interim Measures for Generative AI take effect, marking China's first binding regulation on public generative AI. Providers must file algorithms, conduct security assessments, and uphold state values.

title: US Executive Order 14110
description: President Biden signs a sweeping, landmark Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI, using the Defense Production Act to require safety reporting from foundational model developers.

title: First REAIM Summit (The Hague)
description: The inaugural Summit on Responsible Artificial Intelligence in the Military Domain (REAIM) is co-hosted by the Netherlands and the Republic of Korea, launching a global dialogue on military AI norms.

title: Bletchley Declaration
description: At the first global AI Safety Summit held at Bletchley Park, 28 countries including the US, EU, and China sign a declaration acknowledging severe frontier AI risks and committing to global cooperation.

title: IndiaAI Mission Launched
description: The Indian government launches the 'IndiaAI Mission' to provide advanced infrastructure, compute access, and significant support for homegrown startups and researchers.

title: First UN General Assembly AI Resolution
description: The UN General Assembly unanimously adopts a landmark resolution promoting safe, secure, and trustworthy artificial intelligence systems, emphasizing human rights and sustainable development.

title: EU AI Act Enters Into Force
description: The EU AI Act officially enters into force, establishing the world's first comprehensive legal framework for AI and triggering the countdown for the phased enforcement of specific regulations.

title: Second REAIM Summit (Seoul)
description: Hosted by the Republic of Korea, the second REAIM Summit convenes international defense and policy leaders to establish concrete normative frameworks and governance mechanisms for AI in the military domain.

title: Brazil Senate Approves AI Bill
description: The Brazilian Senate approves Bill no. 2338/2023, setting rules for the ethical development and use of AI in Brazil, moving the country closer to comprehensive national AI regulation.

title: EU AI Act: Prohibitions Take Effect
description: Prohibitions on 'unacceptable risk' AI systems defined by the EU AI Act—such as real-time biometric categorization, workplace emotion inference, and social scoring—become legally enforceable.

title: UNIDIR AI, Security and Ethics (#AISE25)
description: The inaugural UNIDIR conference in Geneva convenes to address destabilization, lifecycle management, and data practices regarding AI's application for security and defence.

title: EU AI Act: GPAI Obligations Apply
description: Obligations for General-Purpose AI (GPAI) models become applicable. Providers of foundation models must adhere to transparency requirements, copyright laws, and risk governance frameworks.

title: Rwanda National AI Agency
description: Rwanda officially approves the establishment of a National AI Agency, becoming the first African country with a dedicated, standalone national institution to scale its AI policy framework.

title: UNIDIR AI, Security and Ethics (#AISE26)
description: Following UN General Assembly resolutions 79/239 and 80/58, UNIDIR bridges technical and policy communities to implement governance frameworks and practical measures for military AI.

title: EU AI Act: General Application Date
description: The general application date for the majority of the EU AI Act. Requirements for high-risk AI systems become enforceable, alongside transparency rules for deepfakes and generative content.

title: EU Digital Omnibus Regulation
description: Under the EU Digital Omnibus, strict rules and conformity assessments for certain high-risk areas, including complex biometrics and border control, go into full application.

title: EU AI Act: Embedded System Compliance
description: The final major phase of the EU AI Act roll-out. High-risk AI systems embedded into regulated physical products, such as medical devices, lifts, and toys, are required to be fully compliant.
